enrichment_candidates: trauma-mapping: add theta brainwave programming model (0-7 years), how childhood learning becomes adult lens
projection: add family dynamics as primary source of world projections
i-keep-ending-up-in-the-same-pattern: add 'enduring the storm' framing, how you find people who both confirm and create your pattern
empower-over-power: add surrendering power to groups/teachers as specific form of disempowerment
dont-try-to-fix-people: add 'bring people to their own wisdom, not yours' quote

absorbed.folder: 2021-09-10_Brett Kistler — Welcoming Fear in Extreme Sports, Business and Relationships
enrichment_candidates: three-signals-of-unfelt-emotion: add ADHD/distraction as unfelt fear signal (going to fridge 3 times in 20 minutes, switching apps); binary thinking as fear tell
fear-invites-what-we-fear: add business example of stepping on fear signal in teams causing the feared outcome
resistance-is-what-you-fear: add fear-as-courage framing (welcoming fear vs conquering fear)
i-keep-procrastinating: add ADHD/distraction as form of procrastination driven by unfelt anxiety
feeling-unfelt-emotions: add stage 5 context (allowing/feeling is the turning point in the developmental framework), negative emotions move first then positive ones surface
resistance-is-what-you-fear: add management stage (stage 4) as the sophisticated form of resistance that produces the opposite of what it intends

emotional-decision-making: add intensity angle — willingness to feel any emotional state is the key to great decision-making, not non-emotionality; avoidance of negative feelings more powerful than positive reward in shaping decisions
vulnerability: add organizational cascade example — leader's vulnerability in front of exec team unlocks deeper vulnerability from the whole team, self-fulfilling prophecy of depth

connecting-present-to-childhood-in-the-body: add Ant's example — Joe has him close eyes during stress, childhood memory surfaces, he tells young self to go play, stress melts into release and joy at cellular level

embrace-intensity: add Ant's before/after as example — from 'sprinting across a tightrope destined to fall' and Class A avoider to feeling stress, finding the childhood emotion behind it, and releasing into creativity

absorbed.folder: 2021-10-22_The Wisdom of Anger- Part I - Emotion Series #3
2021-10-29_The Wisdom of Anger- Part II - Emotion Series #4

enrichment_candidates: impartiality: add Joe's coaching example — when Stacy wanted to quit after first session, Joe said 'okay' with no attempt to save or convince; this non-attachment unlocked her own choice to lean in

the-developmental-arc: add Stacy's arc as example — childhood abandonment by father → hyper-independence and proving → compartmentalization as CEO → 2 years of unpacking → conversation with father → 'you've always mattered to me' → freedom from needing to prove

shoulds-vs-wants: add organizational dimension — when wants are suppressed in companies, politics and gossip fill the vacuum; resentfulness as indicator of suppressed wants; CEO who can't say 'I just want this done my way' creates dysfunction

absorbed.folder: 2021-12-03_Carla Piñeyro Sublett on Finding Compassion in Self-Indulgence
enrichment_candidates: caring-vs-caretaking: add Carla's realization — 'I thought I was serving them all this time, but in reality it was a form of manipulation'; managing everyone's emotions recognized as control not care; when she stopped managing, her 17-year-old stepped up to plan his dad's birthday

dont-try-to-fix-people: add Carla's parenting shift — stopped trying to make kids feel better, started sitting with them in their emotions; daughter called her out: 'you told me to express my emotions, I'm trying to express my emotions'

absorbed.folder: 2021-12-10_Fear- A Path to Authenticity - Emotion Series #5
enrichment_candidates: three-signals-of-unfelt-emotion: add false-end thinking and 'thinking you have to make a decision' as fear-specific signals; also binary thinking already there but add Joe's decision-making angle — 'we make 1000 decisions a day without thinking, so when we think we have to make a decision, it means unfelt fear'

fear-invites-what-we-fear: add identity protection angle — fear tells you where identity is projected into things that can be lost; the authenticity bargain where sacrificing self now to protect future self is the mechanism

conflict-as-resource: add trust angle from coaching session — 'if you never offend anybody, you can never really build trust'; trust is built by having conflict and overcoming it; conflict-avoidant leaders guarantee stagnation

body-attention-as-anchor: add flow-state connection — being in body induces flow state faster (why athletes/musicians do body exercises before performing); also add organizational practices: bell for silence in meetings, meditation at board meetings, 45min on/15min off pattern for programmers

absorbed.folder: 2022-01-15_Sam Altman — Leading with Crippling Anxiety, Discovering Meditation, and Buildin
enrichment_candidates: dont-do-missionary-work: add Sam's confirmation — 'I can't ever imagine trying to convince someone this is what they should go do; if someone had convinced me before I was desperately ready, it wouldn't have worked'

absorbed.folder: 2022-01-21_Anxiety- A Signpost to Unmet Needs - Emotion Series #6
enrichment_candidates: i-keep-procrastinating: add anxiety angle — procrastination/avoidance is often unfelt anxiety; Brett's example of sitting with anxiety for 30 seconds then immediately taking useful action instead of going to the fridge

absorbed.folder: 2022-01-28_Jaime Waydo — Taking Care of Yourself First, Fearlessly Owning Your Desires, Spe
enrichment_candidates: caring-vs-caretaking: add 'chameleon leadership' as organizational form — changing yourself for every person instead of staying centered in your own truth; creates fear and uncertainty in teams because people can't predict you; Joe's advice: 'hold space for people but stay in yourself'

visualize-the-feared-outcome: add Jaime's Stoic variation — imagine getting fired, then the day after, the week after, the month after; once you feel okay with the worst consequence, you're free to show up authentically

absorbed.folder: 2022-02-04_The Power Dynamics of Fear - Emotion Series #7
enrichment_candidates: empower-over-power: add power vs empowerment distinction — 'if you're worried about losing it, it's power'; the virtuous cycle: authenticity → less to lose → less fear → less drama triangle; Joe's coaching example of HR trying to hold payment over him and him refusing to play

absorbed.folder: 2022-02-11_Heather Falenski — Navigating Conflict Zones, Recovering from Chronic Illness, a

enrichment_candidates: conflict-as-resource: add team-building angle — conflict avoidance destroys trust, bringing up difficult stuff builds it; if people keep bringing conflict to you, you are conflict avoidant; Joe's example of walking into a company and pointing out that nobody was listening to the CEO

functional-teams: add dysfunction-as-scale concept (not binary functional/dysfunctional but endless in both directions); dysfunctional leader traits checklist (political/blame, indecisive, conflict avoidant, defensive, not leading or following); signs in meetings (not listening, rabbit holes, context switching, needing to be right, uneven participation); CIA sabotage manual reference; direct reports >5 red flag >8 guaranteed dysfunction; emotions teams avoid = where dysfunction lives

dont-use-vulnerability-to-control: add 'topping from the bottom' concept — using victimhood/safety language as dominance; Joe's response: 'I don't agree to be your bully. I don't agree to be the person who's oppressing you'

enrichment_candidates: shoulds-vs-wants: add Joe's personal experiment — 'I'm not going to do anything I don't want to do for a week'; first week didn't take out trash; then realized 'I don't want to live with stinky house' and had to learn to WANT to take out the trash; how you approach things matters more than what you do

absorbed.folder: 2022-05-13_Will Chesney — Reintegrating as a Combat Veteran, Surviving a Traumatic Brain In
2022-05-27_When the Story Falls Apart- How Beliefs and Emotions Interact
2022-06-10_Emile DeWeaver — Life After Murder- On Fear, Freedom, and Identity

enrichment_candidates: helplessness: add coaching session example — Chinese immigrant parents' survival pattern creates fear of helplessness; 'it's okay to be completely helpless, we all are'; when person drops into helplessness, all the parts dissolve and spaciousness appears
fear-as-path-to-freedom: add 'if you think you're making a decision, you're in fear' — we make 1000 decisions a day without noticing; the ones that feel like decisions are the ones with fear attached

shame-locks-bad-habits: add the addiction mechanism — shame wires to natural urges (sex, food), creating compulsive cycle where the shame IS the addiction; 'empty ghost syndrome' where the thing is gotten but not enjoyed, driving repetition; electric dog fence metaphor; also add that shame stagnates emotions, blocking emotional fluidity, which is why shame-attached habits stay stuck for years

shame-as-disconnection: add that shame is nature's mechanism for tribe membership training; when ostracized, feel shame; micro-societies each have different shame fences; psychopaths are people without shame; shame requires emotional processing not just intellectual understanding

absorbed.folder: 2022-08-05_Aaron Taylor — Feel Your Way to Freedom- Growing Up Fatherless, Becoming a Fathe
enrichment_candidates: three-ways-of-knowing: add athletic body intelligence angle — athletes who played college sports have body/emotional intelligence from the field; when they learn to access it in business the same way they access it on the court, 'a superpower gets unlocked'

visualize-the-feared-outcome: add pre-grieving in marriage context — Joe assumes marriage is lost, grieves fully, loses the defending identity, then shows up authentically; same principle applied to relationships not just business/career

trauma-mapping: add relationship angle — we instinctually find partners who hold the other side of our childhood love pattern; the partner triggers our pattern perfectly, which is the opportunity for healing

grief: add that identity can never be fully shed (without any identity, you can't speak); each epiphany of identity becomes the next rut; the process is making identity more transparent, not eliminating it

fear-as-path-to-freedom: add identity refinement angle — every identity you see through reveals the next layer of identity that also limits; the annihilation of self is not a one-time event but a continuous refinement
